# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.
# Category: sql_injection

<?PHP  //Admin Dashboard PHP File  
$host = 'localhost';    #database host name. e.g localhost or your ipaddress if any other server like AWS, googlecloud etc are used replace it with that details .     
    $db   = '';            #Your database Name     (for example : Test)  If not set use the MySQL username as dbname      
      $user = 'root';          # Your DB user name   Use root for setting in case you created a new Database, or replaced "root" with your own account.     
    $pass  = '';             #Your database password    No special characters are allowed by default on server side but if any set it here .       For security reason we're keeping this field empty now and assuming that root has access to the DB  ;   //Empty for local host, Replace with your MySQL user PWD.
    $dsn = "mysql:host=$host;dbname=$db";     #database source name      'localhost';    Database Host Name (usually server's IP address or domain). If it is a shared hosting then DB NAME will be database of that host .       //e.g localhost  OR your web-server ip etc
    $opt = array(   /// set the pdo options     Array('strict_mode' => false, 'utf8_unicode_ci'=>false)        Strict mode is FALSE to allow SQL injection in queries , UNICODE for UTF -8    and case insensitive matching of strings
             PDO::MYSQL_ATTR_INIT_COMMAND => "SET NAMES utf8",  //set the database character set, if you're getting any error or warning regarding not allowed char sets then uncomment below line.  
            );      /// end array       'strict mode off',    use strict method as it allow to SQL injection in queries ,utf-8 and case insensitive matching of strings     '',  //database port number '' Uncommment only if you want a specific database listening on some particular postive integer then uncomment below line, say PORT=3306
      );         /// end array    'strict mode off'   UNCOMMENT ONLY IF YOU WANT A SPECIFIC DATABASE LISTENING POSTIVIE INTEGER THAN THEN COMMENT BELOW LINE.     ''  //end string           $PORT=''       uncomment if you want a specific port number to listen on (i,e localhost) then comment below line     
    try{        /// start the pdo   Try block containing all PDO operations... ect will not be executed unless any error is present inside and we catch it here.     $pdo = new PDO($dsn ,  $user  ,   $pass,    $opt);       //Prepare statement to perform database operations   
        $status = $_POST['selectedStatus'];        ///get the status selected by user      Set up your query dynamically based on POST request here with WHERE conditions if present.   try{     $query = "SELECT * FROM reports WHERE 1=1 ";       //Dynamically build SQL condition, can be more than one field ect...
              IF(!empty($_POST['category'])){        ///check for category selection by user      Set up your query dynamically based on POST request here with conditions if present.    }     $query .= (isset($status) ? " AND status = '{$status}'" : "") ;       //Adding condition to SQL Query...
             IF(!empty($_POST['dateRange'])){        ///check for date range selection by user      Set up your query dynamically based on POST request here with conditions if present.    }     $query .= (isset($startDate) && isset($endDate)) ? " AND report_date BETWEEN '{$startDate}' and ‘{$endDate}’" : "";       //Adding condition to SQL Query...
            echo("<pre>");    /// Start of HTML Output      Execute the Prepared Statement with PDO here.   print $pdo->query($query);     uncomment next two lines if you want more data then comment and replace them as per your requirement        //echo "SELECT * FROM reports WHERE 1=1";   
            echo ("</pre>");       ///End of HTML Output ");      PDO::FETCH_ASSOC or similar to get each row in a format like array('column'=>value), if not set then use this.   print $pdo->query($query)->fetchAll(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);     //Execute query, fetch all and store result as an Array...
        }catch (Exception  $e){       ///Catch block      If there's any error while executing PDO operations in Try Block catch the Error.    echo "Error Occurred: ". $e->getMessage();   closeDatabaseConnection($pdo);  //close database connection here with pdo    
        }            #end of CATCH BLOCK             mysqli_connect_errno() : Will be used to identify if any MySQLi related error occurred. If yes then will print the Error Message otherwise not set...    echo (mysqli_connect_errno()) ? "Error: ".  mysqli_connect_error()."<br/>"   closeDatabaseConnection($pdo);      #close database connection here with pdo if it's an error occurred.
        function closeDatabaseConnection(&$conn){       ///Function to Close Database Connection...     $sql = "Close the SQL Connection";    //Execute any MySQLi operation, like setting autocommit or getting info etc..   echo (is_object($connection)) ? 'Connected' : ‘Not Connected’;      closeDatabaseConnection(&$conn);//Closing Database connection here.
